title: Where-clause validator mishandles TP doubled-quote escaping breaking injection defense and valid queries
description: The where-clause splitter in src/api/query/query-builder.ts:258-278 toggles inQuote on unescaped quotes only, but TP escapes single quotes by doubling them. A name value containing an apostrophe is silently split into two clauses then rejected as malformed by the downstream regex at line 293. The sanitizer both lets injection payloads through and rejects valid input containing apostrophes. Fix: implement TP-aware doubled-quote parsing in validateWhereClause.

title: TQL injection via user-supplied filter values in operations layer
description: User input is string-concatenated directly into TQL where-clauses without escaping in src/operations/general/search-work-items.ts:43 and src/operations/work/show-my-tasks.ts:80. A value containing a single quote can append arbitrary clauses and broaden queries beyond the intended scope. The validator in query-builder.ts is the intended safety net but is itself broken by a companion bug. No tests cover these injection vectors. Fix: escape single quotes as doubled single quotes in QueryBuilder.formatWhereValue and stop string-concatenating user input in operations.

title: Attachment binary download silently fails auth in API-key mode
description: src/api/http/http-client.ts:231-273 downloadBinary builds headers via buildHeaders() which emits no auth header in API-key mode since TP uses access_token as a query param. Attachment URIs from /Attachments/{id} are not guaranteed to include the token. Result: analyze-attachment silently fails in the most common deployment mode. Fix: inject access_token query param into binary download URLs when in API-key mode.

title: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests treated as terminal error with no retry
description: src/api/http/http-client.ts:130-136 short-circuits retry for all 4xx responses including 429. Rate limit responses surface as generic McpError(InvalidRequest) with no retry hint to callers. Fix: detect 429 specifically, honor Retry-After header, and apply exponential backoff before surfacing the error. 5xx responses already retry correctly.

title: Query validator operator set contradicts project documentation and example queries
description: The validator regex in src/api/query/query-builder.ts:293 only accepts aliased operators (eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, contains) but CLAUDE.md and resource-provider.ts document symbolic forms (=, !=, >, <, >=, <=). Parenthesized example queries like (Project.Id = 123) cannot pass validation. Fix: align the validator to accept TP actual query grammar or update all documentation to match the validator.

title: [HIGH] Fix delete auth bypass when User is null
description: delete-comment.ts:53 — Invert guard to fail closed. If getCommentContext() returns null or has no .User, deny deletion for non-managers. Currently the optional chaining skips the entire auth check.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed for null/missing comment context. Non-managers get error response on API failure, Unauthorized when comment not found or has no User. Managers bypass both checks. Fixed pre-existing test conflict (block vs warn for other users' comments).

title: Security review remediation
description: Resolve all findings from the cross-reviewed security audit: 1 High, 2 Medium, 7 Low. See SECURITY-REVIEW.md and plan at .claude/plans/sunny-whistling-lecun.md
close_reason: All child issues resolved. 8 fixes applied, 1 won't-fix (hostname in SSRF error), 1 accepted limitation (DNS rebinding). SECURITY-REVIEW.md and SECURITY-REMEDIATION.md committed.

title: Comment hierarchy orphan threading may silently drop or flatten deep replies
description: src/api/comments/comment.service.ts:305-331 dumps orphan comments at end unsorted and per-parent sort is shallow. Replies to orphans may be dropped or flattened depending on getCommentReplies vs getComments semantics. Investigate whether TP API flattens deeply nested replies and handle or document the behavior explicitly.

title: validateEntityId accepts integers beyond TP server int32 limit causing opaque 400 errors
description: src/api/validation/entity-validator.ts:135-149 accepts any positive integer up to Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. TP IDs are int32 server-side so values above 2147483647 produce opaque 400 errors from the server. Fix: add an upper bound check of 2147483647 to validateEntityId with a clear error message.

title: validateEntityId bypassed by most operations allowing invalid IDs to reach API layer
description: Most operations pass entityId directly to service.getEntity or updateEntity without invoking EntityValidator. Most schemas do not enforce .positive() so zero and negative integer values reach the API layer unchecked. Fix: call validateEntityId consistently in all operations before any API calls and add .positive() constraints to all entityId schema fields.

title: Shallow test coverage leaves most operations and all injection vectors untested
description: Only search.tool and get.tool have tests covering happy paths. Untested: create.tool, update.tool, inspect.tool, comment.tool, show-my-tasks, show-my-bugs, start-working-on, complete-task, log-time, search-work-items, analyze-attachment. targetprocess.test.ts contains only expect(true).toBe(true). The TQL injection vectors have zero test coverage. Fix: add tests for all operations including injection inputs, apostrophe values, and edge-case entity IDs.

title: EntityRegistry mutated on every metadata refresh without idempotency guard
description: src/api/client/tp.service.ts:380-383 calls EntityRegistry.registerCustomEntityType on every cache refresh cycle. If registerCustomEntityType is not idempotent, registered types accumulate unboundedly across tenant entity type churn. Fix: verify idempotency in core/entity-registry.ts and add a guard if the method does not already deduplicate registrations.

title: No maximum length validation on free-text input fields allows oversized payloads
description: searchToolSchema.where, add-comment comment field, complete-task summary, analyze-attachment description, and other free-text fields have only min(1) or no length constraint. Oversized payloads are sent to TP API and can be persisted server-side. Fix: add reasonable max length constraints to all free-text Zod schema fields.

title: orderBy direction parameter silently stripped with no warning to caller
description: src/api/query/query-builder.ts:320 and src/tools/search/search.tool.ts:90 strip asc/desc suffix from orderBy fields with no warning or error. Users who provide direction keywords receive silently unsorted or default-sorted results. Fix: either accept and pass through direction keywords per TP REST capability, or reject them with an explicit error message.

title: Time-entry date parser throws TypeError on unexpected date formats
description: src/operations/work/log-time.ts:210 and :223 call entry.Date.match(/\d+/)[0] without null-checking the result. When entry.Date is not in /Date(123)/ format, match() returns null and [0] throws TypeError. The surrounding catch at line 139 only covers the search call not the in-loop reduce. Fix: add null check before array access and handle unexpected date formats gracefully.

title: Config loading lacks schema validation and has insecure path fallbacks
description: src/server.ts:84 parses credential JSON with JSON.parse and no Zod validation, no file permission check, and no warning for world-readable files. src/server.ts:62-64 builds a HOME-relative path that resolves to /.targetprocess.json when HOME is unset in misconfigured containers. Fix: validate config with a Zod schema, warn when config file permissions exceed 0600, and skip the HOME-relative fallback when HOME is empty.

title: Auth credentials accessible via TypeScript private bypass using bracket notation
description: src/api/client/tp.service.ts:481 accesses this.queryBuilder[authConfig] bypassing TypeScript private visibility. QueryBuilder also exposes a public getAuthConfig() method. Any future consumer can extract basic-auth credentials or API keys from memory. Fix: use ECMAScript private fields (#authConfig) or remove the public accessor.

title: Hand-rolled Zod-to-JSON-Schema converter produces incorrect MCP tool contracts
description: src/server.ts:303-336 walks zodSchema._def shape and only handles ZodNumber, ZodBoolean, ZodEnum. Optionals, arrays, unions, and discriminated unions collapse to string or are dropped entirely. MCP clients receive wrong input schemas and cannot precheck arguments. comment.tool.ts:90-159 has the same bug with a flat schema representing a discriminated union. Fix: replace the bespoke walker with the zod-to-json-schema package.

title: Pervasive use of any type defeats TypeScript safety in API critical paths
description: Multiple files use any where proper types exist: tp.service.ts:49 casts config as any after discriminator already proved the type; comment.service.ts:123 uses commentData any; fetchMetadata, getAttachmentInfo, downloadAttachment all return Promise<any>; context-builder.ts:51-112 maps with (item: any); operations cast service results as any. One TP schema change silently corrupts context with no runtime error. Fix: replace any with proper interfaces or unknown with runtime guards.

title: MIME type validation fails-open when attachment MimeType or Name fields are missing
description: In src/operations/general/analyze-attachment.ts:117, the MIME type guard only runs if MimeType is truthy, so null or undefined bypasses the allowlist check silently. Same pattern at line 128 for suspicious filename detection. A TP server with stale metadata bypasses both controls. Fix: fail closed when MimeType or Name is missing or empty and return a clear error rather than proceeding with an unknown file type.

title: Fix delete-comment.test.ts: wasOwnComment owner field mismatch
description:
1 test in operations/delete-comment.test.ts fails due to an ownership comparison bug.

## Problem
Test scenario: comment owner is 'Test User', current user is also 'Test User'. Test expects `wasOwnComment: true` in result metadata. Actual value is `false`.

The DeleteCommentOperation ownership check is comparing the wrong field — likely `Owner.Id` vs `Owner.FullName`, or `Owner.Login` vs `Owner.FullName`. The mock data supplies a specific field that the comparison logic does not use.

## What needs to change

1. Inspect the ownership comparison in `DeleteCommentOperation` (likely in `src/operations/`)
2. Identify which field the mock supplies for the owner identity vs which field the comparison reads
3. Align the comparison to use the same field the test mock provides — likely a one-line fix

## Acceptance
- The wasOwnComment test in delete-comment.test.ts passes
- No other test suite regresses
close_reason: Fixed wasOwnComment comparison by adding name-based fallback. When TP_USER_ID is non-numeric (parseInt returns NaN), the ID comparison always fails; falling back to FirstName+LastName vs context.user.name handles that correctly.

title: Fix add-comment.test.ts: update assertions to match <!--markdown--> behavior
description:
4 tests in operations/add-comment.test.ts are failing due to wrong expectations about output format and developer suggestions.

## Problem

### Failures 1-3: Markdown-to-HTML conversion
Tests expect server-side HTML output (`<strong>`, `<em>`, `<table>`, `data-language` attributes). The implementation intentionally sends raw markdown with a `<!--markdown-->` prefix and delegates rendering to the TP client.

Decision: Update tests to match current behavior (no server-side conversion).

### Failure 4: start-working-on in developer suggestions
Test asserts that `start-working-on` appears in the developer role suggestion list after adding a comment. It is not present. Current suggestions are intentional.

Decision: Remove or update the `start-working-on` assertion.

## What needs to change (src/__tests__/operations/add-comment.test.ts only)

1. Change markdown assertions to check for `<!--markdown-->` prefix followed by raw markdown (e.g. `**Bold**`, `*italic*`, backtick code) instead of HTML tags
2. Remove the `expect(...).toContain('start-working-on')` assertion (or replace with an assertion about what IS in the suggestion list)

No source code changes needed.

## Acceptance
- All 4 previously-failing tests in add-comment.test.ts pass
- No other test suite regresses

title: Fix tp.service.test.ts: switch to native fetch mock, fix constructor + baseUrl
description:
22 tests in tp.service.test.ts are failing because the wrong HTTP client is being mocked.

## Problem
Tests mock `axios`, but HttpClient uses `node-fetch`. Every mock is a no-op; real HTTP requests fire and fail. Comment method tests try `globalThis.fetch` but HttpClient imports fetch from node-fetch (not globalThis), so those also miss.

Secondary issues:
- `baseUrl` assertion checks `(service as any).baseUrl` but the property lives inside httpClient, not TPService directly
- `new TPService({ domain })` (no credentials) crashes with a null-read instead of the expected validation message

## What needs to change

### Source changes
1. `src/api/http/http-client.ts` — remove node-fetch import, use globalThis.fetch (native in Node v18+)
2. `src/api/comments/comment.service.ts` — same
3. `src/api/tp.service.ts` — add guard at top of constructor: if neither credentials nor apiKey provided, throw with message 'Either credentials or apiKey must be provided'

### Test changes (src/__tests__/api/tp.service.test.ts)
4. Replace all `jest.mock('axios')` sections with globalThis.fetch mocking pattern already used in comment method tests
5. Fix baseUrl assertion to use the correct accessor path (httpClient getter or updated path)
6. Remove the two `validateWhereClause` describe blocks — method moved to QueryBuilder; codebase precedent is to remove private method tests and rely on public API coverage

## Acceptance
- All 22 previously-failing tests in tp.service.test.ts pass
- No other test suite regresses

title: Evaluate and synthesize Claude Code findings
description: Review findings from the 3 research agents and create a comprehensive summary report. Analyze patterns, identify most impactful changes, compare feature importance, and provide actionable insights about Claude Code's evolution.

title: Investigate Claude Code CLI/Command features (last 4 weeks)
description: Research and document new CLI commands, command options, and terminal features released in the past 4 weeks. Focus on: new slash commands, command improvements, workflow enhancements, automation capabilities, hook features.

title: Investigate Claude Code integrations & platform features (last 4 weeks)
description: Research and document new integration capabilities, MCP tools, skill system features, and platform connections released in the past 4 weeks. Focus on: new MCP servers, skill improvements, IDE integrations, API enhancements, authentication/configuration features.

title: Investigate Claude Code UI/UX features (last 4 weeks)
description: Research and document new features in Claude Code's interface, user experience improvements, and visual enhancements released in the past 4 weeks. Focus on: editor improvements, UI changes, interactive features, accessibility updates.

title: Generate SECURITY-REMEDIATION.md
description: After all fixes, generate markdown doc with each finding, severity, before/after code, and verification steps

title: [MED] URL-encode token in buildQueryStringManual
description: query-builder.ts:171 — Add encodeURIComponent to access_token value, matching other code paths

title: [MED] Harden mention sanitization
description: add-comment.ts:491 — Replace single-quote escaping with character allowlist: mention.replace(/[^A-Za-z0-9 .\-_@]/g, '')

title: Inspector doc search passes user term to shell script without input validation
description: src/tools/inspect/inspect.tool.ts:295-297 uses execFile which is safe and does not shell-expand arguments. However resources/target-process-docs/search-docs.sh should be audited to confirm it does not internally pipe the user argument through a shell command. Verify and add input sanitization if needed.

title: log-time persists date as UTC midnight causing wrong calendar day in non-UTC timezones
description: src/operations/work/log-time.ts:87 parses YYYY-MM-DD with new Date() which treats the string as UTC midnight per spec. Users in UTC+ timezones will have time entries recorded on the previous calendar day. Fix: parse date as local midnight using a timezone-aware approach or document the UTC requirement explicitly.

title: linkedCommit user input echoed unfiltered into LLM-facing suggestion strings
description: src/operations/general/add-comment.ts:848 interpolates params.linkedCommit into suggestion strings without sanitization. This follows a prompt injection pattern. While not directly exploitable in current usage, it establishes a bad precedent for LLM-facing text construction. Fix: validate or sanitize linkedCommit before interpolation into LLM-facing output.

title: Environment variable coercion treats NaN identically to zero for TP_USER_ID
description: src/server.ts:250-263 uses parseInt(process.env.TP_USER_ID || 0) and checks if (!userId) which matches both NaN and 0 identically. The error message blames a missing variable when the value is present but non-numeric. Same pattern in comment.tool.ts:313. Fix: check explicitly for NaN and provide distinct error messages for missing vs non-numeric values.

title: ResourceProvider documentation teaches example queries that the validator rejects
description: src/resources/resource-provider.ts:142-207 provides example queries using symbolic operators and parentheses that the validator in query-builder.ts:293 rejects. Users following the provided examples get validator errors. Overlaps with the H1 validator grammar issue. Fix: align examples with validator grammar or fix the validator to accept documented syntax.

title: Basic-auth username containing colon misaligns server-side credential parsing
description: src/api/client/tp.service.ts:61 base64-encodes username:password with no guard against colons in the username. Per RFC 7617, colons in usernames break Basic auth parsing. Fix: add validation rejecting usernames containing colons with a clear error message at configuration load time.

title: fetchMetaEndpoint reports malformed JSON for all failure modes masking root cause
description: src/api/client/tp.service.ts:501-510 returns a generic malformed JSON error for 500 responses, network failures, and timeouts alike. Callers cannot distinguish these failure modes. Fix: preserve and surface the original error type in the thrown error message.

title: executeWithRetry retries programmer errors including TypeError and logic errors
description: src/api/http/http-client.ts:123-146 retries on all thrown errors including TypeError thrown inside request closures. Programmer errors should surface immediately rather than burning through 3 retry attempts with exponential backoff. Fix: only retry on network or 5xx errors; rethrow non-McpError non-network errors immediately.

title: No offset or skip parameter in searchEntities prevents deep pagination
description: src/api/client/tp.service.ts:90 only supports take with no skip. Pagination tools operate on cached text rather than server pages making results beyond the first page unreachable via API. Fix: add skip/offset parameter to searchEntities and surface it through the search tool.

title: Context startup probes 10 entity types serially instead of in parallel
description: src/context/context-builder.ts:130-145 awaits entity type probes sequentially. Converting to Promise.all would reduce first-request latency significantly. Not a correctness bug, just an unnecessary performance bottleneck on cold start.

title: [LOW] Verify search-docs.sh argument quoting
description: resources/target-process-docs/search-docs.sh — Read script and verify $1 is properly quoted to prevent argument injection post-execFile fix

title: [LOW] Sanitize attachment paths in markdown
description: add-comment.ts formatPlainTextComment — Strip markdown-special chars from att.description and att.path before interpolation

title: [LOW] Replace bracket notation auth access
description: tp.service.ts:489 — Add getAuthConfig() accessor to QueryBuilder, use it instead of this.queryBuilder['authConfig']

title: [LOW] Fix authUrl fragile ? handling
description: comment.service.ts:53 + tp.service.ts:490 — Return key=value without ? prefix, let authUrl choose separator based on existing query string

title: [LOW] Genericize SSRF error message
description: http-client.ts:238 — Remove configured hostname from error, use generic message
close_reason: Won't fix. TP domain is not secret — it's in env config and known to all MCP users. Informative error messages are more valuable than obscuring a non-sensitive hostname. Cross-reviewers agreed Low severity.

title: [LOW] Strip tokens from retry error context
description: http-client.ts:~266 — Use new URL(url).pathname in downloadBinary retry context string to avoid leaking access_token

title: SIGINT handler closes server immediately without draining in-flight requests
description: src/server.ts:158 closes on SIGINT without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Acceptable for stdio MCP transport. Worth addressing if transport changes to HTTP or SSE where abrupt closure could corrupt responses.

title: Comment deletion manager-role check is purely client-side and misleadingly documented
description: src/operations/general/delete-comment.ts:48-49 reads manager role from TP_USER_ROLE env var. The fail-closed comment is misleading since TP server enforces real authorization independently. The check provides only a UX gate not a security control. Fix: update code comment to clarify this is UX-only and real enforcement is server-side.

title: getValidEntityTypes cache-init uses shape-fragile null-check pattern prone to race conditions
description: Current event-loop semantics keep this safe but inserting an await between the null check and assignment would create a race. The pattern is technically correct today but fragile for future maintenance. Consider converting to a proper once-initialized pattern using a dedicated initialization promise.

title: SVG attachments whitelisted enabling data-URL embedding as potential XSS vector
description: src/operations/general/analyze-attachment.ts:107 includes image/svg+xml in ALLOWED_IMAGE_TYPES and line 234 embeds it as a data URL in Markdown output. SVG supports inline scripts and event handlers. Low priority given current internal-team-only usage. Fix if external users are added: remove SVG from the allowlist or sanitize SVG content before embedding.

title: [LOW] Document DNS rebinding limitation
description: http-client.ts:235 — Add comment noting hostname check is susceptible to DNS rebinding (theoretical, low risk)
